articles.title: Nigeria fuel subsidy protests spread to Abuja as unions join
articles.excerpt: Labour unions announced they would join demonstrations as anger over rising pump prices widened across the capital.

articles.title: Lagos rallies grow over cost of living and fuel prices
articles.excerpt: Thousands gathered in Lagos to demand relief measures, with organisers citing transport and food costs.

articles.title: Nigeria's government defends subsidy removal amid protests
articles.excerpt: Officials said the policy was needed to stabilise public finances, while promising targeted support for the poorest households.

articles.title: Fuel queues return as tensions rise in Nigerian cities
articles.excerpt: Long lines formed at filling stations as distribution disruptions compounded frustration among motorists.

articles.title: Civil society groups call for dialogue over Nigeria fuel crisis
articles.excerpt: Advocacy organisations urged talks between the government and unions to avoid a prolonged shutdown.

articles.title: Explainer: What is behind Nigeria's fuel subsidy dispute
articles.excerpt: A look at the economics of subsidy removal and why it has become a recurring flashpoint.

articles.title: Nigerian students protest transport fare hikes
articles.excerpt: Student groups marched over the knock-on effect of fuel prices on daily commutes.
